reasoning: The NFL Competition Committee chose not to propose a ban on the 'Tush Push' at the March 2024 Annual League Meeting, leaving the play fully legal for the 2024 season. Given that peak controversy surrounding the play occurred during the 2023 season, the failure to secure 24 owner votes for a ban indicates a lack of sufficient support to outlaw it. Furthermore, with the retirement of Eagles center Jason Kelce, the play may be less successful and therefore less controversial in the upcoming season. Reports of a '1-yard behind' rule clarification largely reflect a past memo rather than a new restriction, as it merely enforces existing alignment rules. Because the urgency to ban the play has significantly receded, it is relatively unlikely (though still remotely possible due to aesthetic/injury concerns from Commissioner Goodell and some owners) that a ban or major restriction will be passed at the Spring 2024 or March/May 2025 meetings. Therefore, the probability is roughly 20%.
